questions.q: Who is considered as the founder of Management Thought?
questions.explanation: Taylor is considered as the founder of Management Thought because of his scientific approach to management.

questions.q: What is the primary function of planning in an organization?
opts: (C) To decide on goals and policies
questions.explanation: Planning is the primary function of planning in an organization as it helps to decide on goals and policies.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Principles of Scientific Management'?
questions.explanation: Taylor is the author of the book 'The Principles of Scientific Management'.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Organizational Structure?
questions.explanation: Organizational Structure is designed to define roles and responsibilities within an organization.

questions.q: Who is considered as the 'Mother of Management'?
questions.explanation: Mary Parker Follett is considered as the 'Mother of Management' due to her emphasis on human relations and organizational behavior.

questions.q: What is the primary goal of Organizational Change?
questions.explanation: Organizational Change is aimed at adapting to changing environment and improving organizational performance.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Practice of Management'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is the author of the book 'The Practice of Management'.

questions.q: What is the difference between Planning and Control?
opts: (A) Planning is a short-term process while Control is a long-term process
(B) Planning is a short-term process while Control is a short-term process
(C) Planning is a long-term process while Control is a long-term process
(D) Planning is a short-term process while Control is a long-term process
questions.explanation: Planning is a long-term process while Control is a short-term process.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Industrial Relations'?
questions.explanation: Elton Mayo is known as the 'Father of Industrial Relations' due to his work on human relations and organizational behavior.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Organizational Learning?
questions.explanation: Organizational Learning is aimed at adapting to changing environment and improving organizational performance.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Human Side of Enterprise'?
questions.explanation: Douglas McGregor is the author of the book 'The Human Side of Enterprise'.

questions.q: What is the difference between Organizational Structure and Organizational Design?
opts: (A) Organizational Structure is the physical arrangement of an organization while Organizational Design is the conceptual framework of an organization
(B) Organizational Structure is the conceptual framework of an organization while Organizational Design is the physical arrangement of an organization
(C) Organizational Structure is the long-term arrangement of an organization while Organizational Design is the short-term arrangement of an organization
(D) Organizational Structure is the short-term arrangement of an organization while Organizational Design is the long-term arrangement of an organization
questions.explanation: Organizational Structure is the physical arrangement of an organization while Organizational Design is the conceptual framework of an organization.

questions.q: Who is known as the 'Father of Modern Management'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is known as the 'Father of Modern Management' due to his work on modern management practices.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Organizational Culture?
questions.explanation: Organizational Culture is aimed at adapting to changing environment and improving organizational performance.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Organization of the Future'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is the author of the book 'The Organization of the Future'.

questions.q: What is the difference between Organizational Change and Organizational Development?
opts: (A) Organizational Change is the process of changing an organization's structure while Organizational Development is the process of changing an organization's culture
(B) Organizational Change is the process of changing an organization's culture while Organizational Development is the process of changing an organization's structure
(C) Organizational Change is the process of changing an organization's strategy while Organizational Development is the process of changing an organization's structure
(D) Organizational Change is the process of changing an organization's structure while Organizational Development is the process of changing an organization's strategy
questions.explanation: Organizational Change is the process of changing an organization's culture while Organizational Development is the process of changing an organization's structure.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'Transforming Organizations'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is the author of the book 'Transforming Organizations'.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Organizational Innovation?
questions.explanation: Organizational Innovation is aimed at adapting to changing environment and improving organizational performance.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The New Realities'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is the author of the book 'The New Realities'.

questions.q: What is the difference between Organizational Effectiveness and Organizational Efficiency?
opts: (A) Organizational Effectiveness is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals while Organizational Efficiency is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals in a cost-effective manner

(B) Organizational Effectiveness is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals in a cost-effective manner while Organizational Efficiency is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals
(C) Organizational Effectiveness is the ability of an organization to achieve its short-term goals while Organizational Efficiency is the ability of an organization to achieve its long-term goals
(D) Organizational Effectiveness is the ability of an organization to achieve its long-term goals while Organizational Efficiency is the ability of an organization to achieve its short-term goals
questions.explanation: Organizational Effectiveness is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals while Organizational Efficiency is the ability of an organization to achieve its goals in a cost-effective manner.

questions.q: Who is the author of the book 'The Effective Executive'?
questions.explanation: Peter Drucker is the author of the book 'The Effective Executive'.
